Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given an event log with three cases We need to produce a process tree that captures the behavior Lets analyze the traces Case 1 RA VF AE RMI VF AD SC AC Case 2 RA VF AE AD SC AC Case 3 RA VF AE RMI RMI VF AD SC AC

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Observations All cases start with Receive Application RA then Validate Form VF then Assess Eligibility AE So a sequence RA VF AE

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: After AE there may be a loop In case 1 after AE RMI then VF then AD In case 2 after AE directly AD no RMI In case 3 after AE RMI RMI then VF then AD

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So it seems the pattern is after AE you may enter a loop that consists of doing RMI one or more times and then a VF

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So we have after AE there is an optional subprocess that is a sequence one or more RMIs then VF So we need to model RMI then VF as optional

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: How to model one or more RMIs That could be a loop A common pattern RMI RMI would execute RMI then either exit or do RMI again and loop That yields one or more RMI

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So we can represent one or more RMIs as RMI RMI Then the block is sequence of that and VF Then this block is optional so we need an XOR between tau and that block

Name: Validation - PE
Text: In case 1 after AE we have RMI then VF That is block In case 3 we have RMI RMI VF that is also block since the loop gives two RMIs In case 2 we skip block

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: There is no concurrency true concurrency observed in the log all activities appear in a strict order per case So we do not need parallel operator

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus the process tree Root is a sequence of RA VF AE an XOR between tau and a sequence of a loop one or more RMIs and VF then AD SC AC

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets verify traces Trace 1 RA VF AE RMI VF AD SC AC works by taking the second XOR branch with one RMI Trace 2 RA VF AE AD SC AC works by taking the tau branch Trace 3 RA VF AE RMI RMI VF AD SC AC works by taking the second branch with two RMIs
